THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:DEFENDANT LIBERTY BANK, CONRAD JIMINIZ, RUBY GO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO EMPLOYMENT FORM INTERROGATORIES; FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND THE CONTINUATION OF PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. Off Calendar Jul-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
KATHERINE C. ZARATE, ESQ., (415)391-4800, FOR Defendant/MOVING PARTY, Liberty Bank
ARTHUR KIM, ESQ., (650)224-8629, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, NIRMALA SINGH

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Liberty Bank's Motion To Compel Psychiatric Examination Of Plaintiff is granted. No opposition has been filed. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

Motion For An Order Compelling The Psyciatric Independent Examination Of Pltf
Off Calendar Jun-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Liberty Bank's Motion To Compel Psychiatric Examination Of Plaintiff is ordered off calendar. A hearsay proof of personal service is not competent evidence that the motion was served.. The proof of service must be signed by the person who actually served the motion. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-21-2008 Set For Trial On Jul-20-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jul-06-09 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 611

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Employment Form Interrogatories; Further Responses To Special Interogatories, Further Production Of Documents; Signed Verification And The Continatin Of Pltf'S Depositin
Off Calendar Apr-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Liberty Bank, Conrad Jiminiz, Ruby Go's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Employment Form Interrogatories; Further Responses To Special Interrogatories; Further Production Of Documents; And The Continuation Of Plaintiff's Deposition is granted. Unverified responses are not responses. The court will not determine the adequacy of the supplemental responses on this motion. The court finds that the initial responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff's deposition shall be resumed within 20 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $1,430, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2008 Off Calendar
